title: THE EIGHTH PASSENGER — DIRECTOR'S CUT V4

pod_identity_correlation_08.title: POD IDENTITY CORRELATION
pod_identity_correlation_08.description: Match each pod record with the correct identity clue.

events.text: I need you to understand that I hate saying that now.
Every time I say I found something, the universe takes it as a dare.
A life support module. External. Still transmitting.
At first I thought it was wreckage.
Not a dead blink. A rhythm.
Like someone tapping on the wall from the other side.
Grant used to tap on bulkheads when he was nervous.
Said it helped him 'negotiate with metal.'
Owens said metal had better legal representation.
I do that now. I remember something stupid so I don't have to think about the terrifying thing in front of me.
Please tell me the universe is done being weird for one minute.
Actually don't. I don't believe either of us would survive that much optimism.
I like breathing. I have grown attached to it.
But if there's someone inside, distance won't save them.
I know what you're doing, by the way.
Trying to keep me from rushing in.
Noah used to say I treated caution like an insult.
I told him caution was fine.

events.title: POD MANIFEST
events.content:
Occupant: UNKNOWN
Status: CRITICAL
Life Support: 23%
Signal Origin: External emergency pod
Beacon Integrity: unstable
Manifest Link: corrupted
Visual Feed: unavailable
events.caption: Recovered emergency pod manifest. Occupant identity unavailable.

events.text: I hate how clean those words are.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: They make panic sound like a spreadsheet.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Please tell me I didn't just imagine that.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Because I have imagined voices before.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: You spend long enough alone and the station starts borrowing people you miss.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.title: POD BEACON ORIGIN
events.content:
Beacon Source: External emergency pod
Launch Vector: Unknown
Docking History: no confirmed ECHO dock record
Carrier Trace: 7.14 MHz harmonic residue detected
Movement Pattern: inconsistent with passive drift
events.caption: Recovered beacon origin report. Pod movement appears non-passive.
events.message: [POD BEACON ORIGIN ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: They drift. They tumble. They die quietly.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Please say the engineering version of 'that's bad.'
events.speaker: MAYA

events.title: POD LIFE SUPPORT SCAN
events.subtitle: Measuring oxygen, pressure and occupant telemetry

events.text: [NOTIFICATION QUEUED: POD LIFE SUPPORT SCAN COMPLETE]

events.title: POD LIFE SUPPORT TRACE
events.content:
Current Capacity: 23%
Pressure: unstable but contained
Thermal Control: failing
Occupant Biometric Signal: active
Heartbeat Pattern: irregular
Estimated Failure Window: 24 hours
External Access: possible with relay guidance
events.caption: Life support telemetry recovered from emergency pod.
events.message: [POD LIFE SUPPORT TRACE ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: Because he said every drill assumed everyone would become useful under pressure.
He said most people become louder.
Not easily. Not safely. Honestly, not in any way that makes my survival instincts happy.
I keep saying that because if I stop, I might start thinking about what else could be inside.
Don't make a voice. I was doing better before it had a voice.
You don't get to use that voice on me.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Whatever is inside that pod is not worth the risk.
And before Maya says I sound heartless, I know exactly how I sound. That is why you should listen.
Sometimes something looks like it needs help because that's the only shape it can use to make you open the door.
You only get this polite when you're terrified.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: If this gets us killed, I will write a very passive-aggressive apology note in the afterlife.
If someone is breathing, we try.
That's the rule. Maybe the only one left.
Noah would have said something stupid here.
Something like, 'heroism is just panic with better lighting.'
Then he would have grinned like he'd invented courage.
Not a kind one. But rational.
And since rational decisions keep being the ones that make Maya hate us, I am enjoying this less than usual.

event.text: There is still someone breathing in there, Hale.
event.speaker: MAYA

event.text: Or something making a machine say they are.
event.speaker: HALE

event.text: You know Noah hated being left waiting.
event.speaker: MAYA

events.text: The pod is close enough for diagnostics.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Not close enough to touch, which is probably good because my hands are making stupid decisions without me.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Give me something technical before I start doing something brave and idiotic.

events.title: POD MOVEMENT ANALYSIS
events.content:
Movement Type: active correction bursts
Thruster Signature: minimal
Guidance Source: unknown
External Pull: not detected
Conclusion: pod is adjusting course without visible command authorization
events.caption: Recovered pod movement analysis. Beacon movement is not passive drift.
events.message: [POD MOVEMENT ANALYSIS ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: I know, I know. We are all tired of that word.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: But impossible is still the correct technical term for 'I hate this.'
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Noah used to argue with autopilot systems.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.title: SUIT ID RECOVERY
events.subtitle: Extracting identifier from pod telemetry and pressure seals

events.title: POD SUIT IDENTIFIER
events.content:
Recovered Suit ID: NV-08
Visual Feed: unavailable
Suit Registry Match: partial
Archive Match: Noah Voss
Identifier Integrity: 72%
Warning: suit identity may not equal occupant identity
events.caption: Suit identifier recovered from emergency pod telemetry.
events.message: [POD SUIT IDENTIFIER ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: He used to write his initials on everything.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: NV on tool tags. NV on cable caps. NV on ration boxes.
Owens said it made him look like a child going to camp.
He said it made him prepared.
But suit identity may not equal occupant identity.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: No. Manifest unknown, suit ID NV-08, life signs active.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Keep the records separate or the pod wins.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: I hate saying that. I hate that it sounds accurate.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: Do not turn a suit into a person just because we want a person.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: The pod remains silent for exactly three seconds.
events.speaker: SYSTEM

events.text: For a moment, all you hear is her trying not to answer.
events.speaker: SYSTEM

events.text: That suit belonged to Noah Voss.
His psych file was missing because there was not supposed to be a file anymore.
At least... that's what we were told.
I am getting very tired of sentences that start that way.
But every time the lights came back on, there he was, butchering some old Earth song like it personally owed him money.
If that's really his voice in that pod...
I don't know what I'll do.
I remember the frost on the visor.
I remember thinking no one should look that peaceful in a place like this.
And if that thing is using his suit ID, then it is not asking for help. It is baiting us.
Please understand how much I want to be wrong.
That was his way of liking people.
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: Most days I needed the second one more.

events.title: DEATH RECORD: NOAH VOSS
events.content:
Subject: Noah Voss
Status: deceased
Reported Date: before Incident Alpha
Body Recovery: confirmed by Engineering Lead
Witness: Ethan Hale
Conflict: emergency pod currently transmits active biometric signal under suit ID NV-08
events.caption: Recovered death record conflicts with active pod telemetry.
events.message: [VOSS DEATH RECORD ARCHIVED TO DATA BANK]

events.text: No. Don't 'Maya' me. You saw the body?
events.speaker: MAYA

events.text: A suit sealed badly enough that no one should have survived it.
events.speaker: HALE

events.text: That is still not the same thing.
events.speaker: MAYA

event.text: Don't leave me in the dark again.
event.speaker: UNKNOWN

event.text: You said you could hear me.
event.speaker: UNKNOWN

events.text: Her breathing comes through the channel first.
events.speaker: SYSTEM

events.text: Or I know what it wants me to think I know.
events.speaker: MAYA
UNKNOWN
events.text: THEN WHO IS INSIDE THE POD?
Life support trace says active biometric signal.
Don't collapse them into one just because one truth would be easier to survive.
Do not honor him by making one now.

focus: Episode 8 upgraded to full Episode 1 / Episode 4 quality target
Noah Voss becomes remembered as a person, not only a file/suit ID
Maya-Noah memories added: jokes, singing, caution, personal habits
Hale-Noah relationship deepened: conflict, respect, grief, fear
Pod signal escalates across the episode, not only at the end
Maya and Hale conflict sharpened around rescue versus suspicion
Pod identity puzzle remains safe with retry and hint flow
Death record confrontation clarified: Hale saw a suit, not necessarily a body
Final cliffhanger escalated with beacon distance countdown and 'OPEN THE POD'
